title: Bless God, He Went As Soldiers,
content:
Bless God, he went as soldiers,
    His musket on his breast;
    Grant, God, he charge the bravest
    Of all the martial blest.

    Please God, might I behold him
    In epauletted white,
    I should not fear the foe then,
    I should not fear the fight.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "Bless God, He Went As Soldiers,", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
